basics.name: Teo Schnell
basics.label: Aerospace Engineering Student
basics.summary: Aerospace engineering student and robotics leader with hands-on experience in UAV design, autonomous systems, flight control, simulation, manufacturing, and STEM tutoring. Current work spans custom STM32-based flight controllers, RC aircraft modeling, and research in advanced photodetector technologies.

work.name: ATAC
work.summary: Supporting aviation research and data analytics projects involving air traffic operations, machine learning, optimization, and artificial intelligence applications.
highlights: Analyze aviation data and support research related to air traffic operations and decision-making
Develop data-driven algorithms and visualization approaches for aviation performance analysis
Contribute to machine learning, optimization, and AI-focused research efforts in aviation

work.name: University of California Santa Cruz
work.summary: Selected for an NSF-funded Research Experience for Undergraduates program at UC Santa Cruz focused on advanced semiconductor photodetectors and instrumentation research.
highlights: Selected for the UC Santa Cruz Materials Science REU program for Summer 2026
Worked in a university research environment focused on advanced semiconductor photodetector technologies
Engaged in experimental research, data analysis, and technical reporting within a multidisciplinary lab setting

work.name: Cabrillo Robotics
work.summary: Co-founded the Cabrillo Robotics Foundation to provide nonprofit support for the Cabrillo Robotics Club, while leading student robotics programs, outreach, and hands-on engineering development.
highlights: Co-founded the Cabrillo Robotics Foundation to support the Cabrillo Robotics Club
Led club operations, student engagement, project coordination, and technical collaboration
Supported robotics education, mentoring, and community outreach through the foundation and club

work.name: Cabrillo College Physics Learning Center
work.summary: Tutored students in physics and engineering concepts, helping them build confidence and improve performance in core STEM coursework.
highlights: Assisted students in mastering complex physics and engineering concepts
Tutored mechanics, circuits, and thermodynamics coursework in one-on-one and small-group sessions
Supported students in developing problem-solving skills and technical understanding

work.name: Cabrillo College
work.summary: Tutored students in computer science and STEM coursework, including programming, mathematics, and foundational engineering topics.
highlights: Tutored students in C++, Python, MATLAB, RISC-V, and mathematics
Became the youngest CS tutor in Cabrillo College history at age 16
Explained programming and math concepts in one-on-one and small-group sessions to improve student understanding

awards.title: GPA 3.60
awards.summary: Maintained a strong academic record while completing engineering and computer science coursework.

awards.title: FAA Part 107 Certification
awards.summary: In progress; supports UAV operations, flight testing, and aviation-related project work.
